summary: Saudi Aramco is in talks with Ukrainian drone manufacturers SkyFall and Wild Hornets to acquire interceptor drones for oil field protection, while also engaging Phantom Defense for electronic warfare systems. On the battlefield, Russian forces are deploying FPV drones in formations of 3-5 units, with operators struggling against video-based targeting systems. The "Yolka" air defense drone has been conducting kinetic intercepts of Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with multiple successful engagements reported in spring 2026. Meanwhile, Ukrainian forces are increasing FPV drone production, with deliveries in January-February 2026 doubling year-over-year, and are also experimenting with tactics like using FPV drones to disrupt enemy positions before strikes.
